message:
refactor(ssh): migrate SSH subsystem from smol::block_on to runtime::block_on

Continue the smol → promise/runtime migration (started in f45bbe4a for
the scripting/config layer) into the SSH subsystem. All E2E tests and
the SSH example binary now use frankenterm_ssh::runtime::block_on
instead of importing smol directly, ensuring the entire codebase uses
a single async blocking primitive.

- examples/ssh.rs: import and use runtime::block_on
- tests/e2e/agent_forward.rs: 2 test functions migrated
- tests/e2e/sftp.rs: 30 test functions migrated
- tests/e2e/sftp/file.rs: 6 test functions migrated
- Update beads issue tracking state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","title":"[asupersync][network] Complete Unix IPC/socket cutover and invariants","description":"Finalize migration of Unix IPC/socket paths, including server/client flows and error-handling invariants.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All targeted Unix IPC paths use asupersync-compatible abstractions with documented invariants.\n2. Error and timeout behavior is consistent across command and daemon sides, including user-facing CLI/robot/watch control flows.\n3. Unit tests cover connect/bind/read/write/error edge cases and deterministic failure semantics.\n4. Integration tests validate end-to-end IPC protocol correctness under load and failure, including restart and degraded-path behavior.\n5. E2E scripts capture IPC traces and structured diagnostics for replay across user-critical workflows (ft robot state/get-text/send/wait-for/search/events, ft watch, and session/snapshot operations).\n6. Closure requires explicit evidence that IPC migration does not regress user-perceived reliability/latency or operator triage quality.","notes":"IPC is a critical reliability axis; avoid hidden fallbacks that reintroduce old semantics. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralMill","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:16.853224Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T05:34:22.442797Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T05:34:22.442782Z","close_reason":"Validated: 80 IPC unit tests + 6 async unix socket integration tests pass, e2e script syntax valid. Unix IPC/socket cutover complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","ipc","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","unix","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1514,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented progress slice for ft-e34d9.10.4.1 (Unix IPC/socket cutover invariants):

Code changes:
1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
- added `ipc_server_restarts_cleanly_on_same_socket_path`
- added `ipc_bind_with_permissions_applies_mode`
- added `ipc_client_reports_server_closed_without_response`

2. crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_unix_socket.rs
- added `unix_socket_rebind_after_listener_drop_supports_restart`
- added `unix_socket_concurrent_clients_round_trip_frames`

3. tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc.sh (new)
- structured JSONL logging (`timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `input_summary`, `outcome`, `reason_code`, `error_code`, `artifact_path`)
- strict rch preflight and explicit fail on local fallback
- nominal checks + deterministic failure injection + recovery contract checks

Validation run in this env:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_unix_socket.rs crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs  => PASS
- bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc.sh => PASS
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc.sh => expected fast fail: `no reachable rch workers; refusing local fallback`
- rch workers probe --all --json => all workers connection_failed (RCH-E100)

Blockers/open items:
- Could not execute cargo test/check/clippy remotely due active rch outage.
- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` scenario wiring is pending active exclusive reservation conflict (LavenderOwl currently holds that file for ft-e34d9.10.2.3).
","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:16:16Z"},{"id":1515,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Additional completion update for ft-e34d9.10.4.1:

- Wired bead e2e harness into global runner:
  - scripts/e2e_test.sh scenario registry entry: `ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc`
  - added runner: `run_scenario_ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc`
  - added dispatcher case arm

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/e2e_test.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc.sh => PASS
- bash scripts/e2e_test.sh --list | rg ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc => PASS (scenario listed)
- bash scripts/e2e_test.sh --skip-self-check --case ft_e34d9_10_4_1_unix_ipc => fails preflight in this env (`Could not find ft binary`) as expected.

Outstanding blocker unchanged:
- rch workers outage (`RCH-E100` on all workers) blocks offloaded cargo test/check/clippy evidence.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:17:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","title":"[asupersync][network] TCP/TLS/HTTP service and client migration","description":"Migrate TCP/TLS/HTTP surfaces to coherent asupersync-compatible implementations and align policy/backoff behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Targeted client/server paths no longer depend on incompatible runtime assumptions.\n2. TLS and HTTP behaviors (timeouts, retries, backoff) are policy-consistent.\n3. Unit tests cover protocol wrappers, timeout matrices, and retry strategies.\n4. Integration tests validate interoperability and failure behavior.\n5. E2E scripts simulate degraded networks and verify structured error taxonomy/logging.","notes":"Final status (FoggyPeak): TCP/IO dual-compile layer complete and committed (e1d6e1a0). runtime_compat::net::{TcpListener, TcpStream} and runtime_compat::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} now feature-gated for both asupersync and tokio paths. metrics.rs already consuming this abstraction. TLS migration requires tokio-rustls dependency addition. HTTP migration requires fastapi framework replacement (ft-181uk). Both are out of scope for this bead — they need their own migration beads. Closing as complete for the achievable scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireSpring","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:17.571400Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:26:20.732561Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:26:20.732547Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","http","integration-tests","logging","network","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","tls","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1552,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","author":"SapphireSpring","text":"SapphireSpring progress slice: updated crates/frankenterm-core/tests/web.rs to use runtime_compat::{net,io} instead of direct tokio net/io imports for TCP HTTP integration tests, and added stream_fetch_prefix_times_out_on_stalled_body invariant test (stalled body after headers returns within bounded timeout). Local rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on that file passes. Remote validation in progress via rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test web --features web -- --nocapture (active build #23). Will append pass/fail output when remote run completes.","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:54:46Z"},{"id":1553,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","author":"SapphireSpring","text":"Validation blocker update for SapphireSpring slice: kept offload-only policy and attempted remote validation twice via rch. Build #23 (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test web --features web -- --nocapture) ran ~641s then was canceled, exit_code=130. Follow-up build #26 (same target with -- --list compile-focused) also canceled, exit_code=130, due prolonged remote queue contention; no local cargo fallback used. Local non-cargo check rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/web.rs passed. Code edits remain: runtime_compat net/io imports in tests/web.rs + stream_fetch_prefix_times_out_on_stalled_body invariant test.","created_at":"2026-02-26T07:01:31Z"},{"id":1554,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","author":"SapphireSpring","text":"Added e2e harness slice for ft-e34d9.10.4.2: new tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_2_network.sh with structured JSONL logs, runtime_compat network token checks, web degraded-timeout test contract checks, harness registration checks, deterministic failure-injection/recovery checks, and strict rch preflight/no-local-fallback policy. Wired scenario into scripts/e2e_test.sh (registry entry + run_scenario_ft_e34d9_10_4_2_network + dispatch case). Local validation: bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_4_2_network.sh scripts/e2e_test.sh PASS; bash scripts/e2e_test.sh --list includes ft_e34d9_10_4_2_network PASS. Remote cargo validation still blocked by overlong/cancelled rch runs (#23/#26).","created_at":"2026-02-26T07:04:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","title":"[asupersync][network] Timeout/backoff/error taxonomy harmonization","description":"Standardize timeout, backoff, and error classification across runtime-migrated I/O paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define canonical timeout/backoff/error taxonomy and apply to targeted modules.\n2. Eliminate inconsistent error codes/messages for equivalent failure classes.\n3. Unit tests verify taxonomy mapping and hint generation logic.\n4. Integration tests validate consistent behavior across modules/endpoints.\n5. E2E scripts assert user-facing and robot-facing error consistency with structured logs.","notes":"Completed (FoggyPeak): Created network_reliability.rs module with unified timeout/backoff/error taxonomy. NetworkErrorKind (transient/permanent/degraded) classifies I/O errors. TimeoutPolicy provides per-subsystem defaults for 8 subsystems. RetryPolicy with 4 presets (aggressive/standard/patient/no_retry) and configurable exponential backoff with jitter. BackoffCalculator for stateful retry delay computation. IoOutcome wraps raw results with classification. ReliabilityConfig composites timeout+retry per subsystem. 41 unit tests + 13 property tests. Commit 923f4d8c.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyPeak","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:18.211837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:37:36.452121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:37:36.452107Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","network","policy","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","title":"[asupersync][track] Vendored crate runtime harmonization","description":"Harmonize vendored FrankenTerm crates so core and vendored layers share one runtime model.

Scope:
- inventory and migrate smol/async-io/tokio residues in vendored crates
- align mux/ssh/runtime assumptions with core doctrine
- define interop contract across crate boundaries

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Vendored crates have explicit runtime migration map with dependency-aware sequencing.
2. Mux and SSH critical paths run with asupersync-compatible semantics and validated behavior.
3. Core<->vendored async contracts are documented and test-enforced.
4. Add unit/integration tests in vendored surfaces for cancellation, channeling, and I/O behavior.
5. Add e2e cross-layer scenarios with structured logging and drift detection.","notes":"Partial core migration without vendored harmonization creates hidden fault lines. This track ensures the whole stack moves coherently and protects user-visible workflows spanning local mux operations, remote SSH sessions, robot orchestration, and stateful session recovery. Require structured artifact/evidence manifests (logs, traces, command records, outputs) in closure evidence. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SwiftAspen","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:24:37.581241Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:21:56.558125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1615,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SageRaven kickoff (2026-02-28): claimed vendored runtime harmonization track after ft-1i2ge.5.2 validation closure. Initial residue inventory (excluding already in-progress mux_client/mux_pool work): frankenterm/config/src/lib.rs (smol::channel + prelude), frankenterm/config/src/config.rs and config/src/lua.rs (smol::block_on), frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs (async-smol imports + smol::block_on test surfaces), frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs + ssh/tests/e2e/* (smol runtime bindings), frankenterm/scripting/src/lua_engine.rs (smol::block_on), frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs (async_io::block_on re-export), frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs + async_ossl/src/lib.rs (async-io IoSafe compatibility stubs). Next slice: choose non-conflicting runtime abstraction cleanup outside mux_client/mux_pool and validate via rch.","created_at":"2026-02-28T01:33:17Z"},{"id":1617,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SageRaven slice (2026-02-28): removed direct smol runtime blocking from config crate Lua paths by switching to runtime-aware promise block_on. Changes: frankenterm/config/src/config.rs adds cfg(lua) import of promise::spawn::block_on and replaces smol::block_on(...) around eval_async config load; frankenterm/config/src/lua.rs adds promise::spawn::block_on import and replaces smol::block_on(...) in Lua event test harness. Residue check: rg confirms no remaining smol::block_on in frankenterm/config/src. Validation attempt via rch exec cargo check -p config --all-targets --features async-asupersync failed open to local due remote connect failure, local fallback was terminated immediately to honor offload-only policy; no heavy local validation completed under this slice.","created_at":"2026-02-28T01:37:51Z"},{"id":2015,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","author":"PlumDeer","text":"Reopened on 2026-03-08 after bv --robot-alerts flagged this issue as stale in_progress with 7+ days of inactivity and no recent bead-thread progress. Clearing the stale claim so it can be triaged again by active agents.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:01:32Z"},{"id":2028,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","author":"SwiftAspen","text":"SwiftAspen taking a bounded vendored-runtime harmonization slice. Reserved frankenterm/config/src/config.rs, frankenterm/config/src/lua.rs, frankenterm/scripting/src/lua_engine.rs, and frankenterm/scripting/Cargo.toml. Scope: replace remaining direct smol::block_on call sites in vendored Lua config/scripting paths with promise::spawn::block_on so these surfaces use the shared runtime bridge instead of hard-coding smol at the call site. Avoiding core crates and the other vendored tracks for now. Agent Mail registration/inbox/reservations work; direct contact handshakes are currently hitting transient DB errors, so overlap coordination is mirrored here until that recovers.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:21:56Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.1","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Vendored runtime primitive inventory and migration map","description":"Produce a detailed map of async/runtime primitive usage in vendored FrankenTerm crates with migration sequencing and ownership.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inventory all runtime-dependent primitives/usages in vendored crates with owning modules and migration strategy.\n2. Classify each usage by migration mode (direct port, adapter, retire) and risk level.\n3. Map each inventory item to user-facing workflows affected (local mux, remote ssh, event streaming, session restore).\n4. Unit tests validate inventory extraction and classification tooling.\n5. Integration tests cross-check inventory completeness against build graph and crate features.\n6. E2E reporting emits structured logs/artifacts and change deltas suitable for migration tracking.","notes":"This bead prevents hidden runtime islands in vendored code and ties them directly to user-observable functionality and risk. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:18.820781Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:26:44.726091Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:26:44.726072Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","inventory","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1517,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Validation: vendored_migration_map.rs 908 lines, 19 unit+property tests, 16/16 e2e steps. Commit 0696808a.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:26:40Z"}]}
